title: Stoke Rochford Hall Lancaster Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed including pilot Flt Lt. Norman William Guy

external_narratives.summary: Registry entry for the memorial to the crew of Lancaster LM719, located in the grounds of Stoke Rochford Hall. Includes a memorial plaque and tree planted in their honour.
external_narratives.key_details: Memorial registered on the national War Memorials Online database. Located in the grounds of Stoke Rochford Hall, Lincolnshire.

external_narratives.summary: Crash site record for Lancaster LM719 (H4-M) of 1653 HCU, which departed RAF North Luffenham for a cross-country navigation exercise on 28 April 1945. The aircraft flew into a heavy snowstorm and the pilot lost control, crashing at 0320 hours.
external_narratives.key_details: Aircraft code H4-M. Crashed during a NAVEX training exercise in severe weather. The war in Europe was just days from ending when this training accident occurred.

external_narratives.summary: Accident database entry for Avro Lancaster B Mk I LM719, recording the crash on Saturday 28 April 1945 near Stoke Rochford Hall during a training exercise from North Luffenham.
external_narratives.key_details: Aircraft was from 1653 Heavy Conversion Unit. Loss occurred just 10 days before VE Day.

external_narratives.summary: International Bomber Command Centre post documenting the memorial at Stoke Rochford Hall, noting the RCAF connection and the poignancy of a training loss so close to the end of the war.
external_narratives.key_details: Canadian (RCAF) crew. Memorial includes both a plaque and a memorial tree in the hall grounds.

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep
